2009-10-01.title: Troop Levels in Afghanistan Since 2001
2009-10-01.abstract: How the American and NATO commitment in Afghanistan has evolved since 2001.

2009-10-01.title: Iran’s Statement May Offer Window Into Nuclear Efforts
2009-10-01.abstract: The head of Iran’s atomic agency says it will disclose the location of one plant “and others,” raising hopes of a glimpse into a hidden production network.

2009-10-01.title: On Day for China Pride, Little Interest in Ideology
2009-10-01.abstract: China’s Communist Party is throwing itself a huge and carefully choreographed party on Thursday.

2009-10-01.title: Possibility of a Nuclear-Armed Iran Alarms Arabs
2009-10-01.abstract: Though it is uncertain how the confrontation over Iranian nuclear facilities will play out, some in the Persian Gulf region are even envisioning a military strike by the West, or Israel.

2009-10-01.title: As U.S. Plots Iran Strategy, Envoy’s Visit Hints at a Thaw
2009-10-01.abstract: The U.S. and diplomats from five other countries are meeting with Iran Thursday in Geneva, a day after a U.S. visit from Iran’s foreign minister, the first of its kind in a decade.

2009-10-02.title: Iranian Opposition Leader Warns Against Sanctions
2009-10-02.abstract: TORONTO — The top Iranian opposition leader warned ahead of the talks between Iran and major powers in Geneva that tougher sanctions on Iran over its nuclear program could hurt the opposition movement more than the government of President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad.

2009-10-02.title: China Is Wordless on Traumas of Communists’ Rise
2009-10-02.abstract: China basked in its 60th anniversary, but there were no solemn pauses for the millions who died during the civil war or were eradicated during Mao’s drive to consolidate power.

2009-10-02.title: Raise Your Mugs to German Efficiency!
2009-10-02.abstract: Oktoberfest proves that Germans can loosen up and have fun. But, as the festival’s lost and found center shows, they are going to have a system in place first.

2009-10-02.title: U.S. Delays Somalia Aid, Fearing It Is Feeding Terrorists
2009-10-02.abstract: American officials are concerned that United Nations contractors may be funneling American aid to the Shabab, a Somali group with ties to Al Qaeda.

2009-10-02.title: Zimbabwe: Activists Sue Over Torture
2009-10-02.abstract: A prominent human rights campaigner, Jestina Mukoko, and eight other activists are suing the government for $500 million in damages.

2009-10-03.title: Guinea Seethes as a Captain Rules at Gunpoint
2009-10-03.abstract: Capt. Moussa Dadis Camara gave some people hope when he seized power in December, but that optimism is gone.

2009-10-03.title: Authorities in Iran Arrest 18 Students
2009-10-03.abstract: After two protests earlier this week, officials ordered the roundup, which students said might only make tensions worse.

2009-10-03.title: Zimbabwe: Farmers to Get More Seed
2009-10-03.abstract: About 680,000 farmers working small plots of land will get high-quality seed and fertilizer this year from international donors.

2009-10-03.title: Russia: Chechen Chief Leads Fighting
2009-10-03.abstract: Special forces units in Chechnya killed at least eight militants Friday in an operation that was personally commanded by the Chechen president.

2009-10-03.title: Germany: Man With Explosives Is Held
2009-10-03.abstract: In a sign of continuing fears of a possible terrorist attack, the police arrested a man who possessed what appeared to be explosive material and an electronic detonator.

2009-10-04.title: Afghan Says U.N. Inquiry on Election Was Flawed
2009-10-04.abstract: Abdullah Abdullah strongly accused the top U.N. official in Afghanistan of refusing to investigate voting fraud.

2009-10-04.title: Attacks on Remote Posts Highlight Afghan Risks
2009-10-04.abstract: An attack that killed eight Americans underscored the vulnerability of the kind of isolated bases that the top American commander wants to scale back.

2009-10-04.title: Vettel Cruises to Victory and Closes in on Button
2009-10-04.abstract: Sebastian Vettel wins, Jenson Button loses Japanese Grand Prix.

2009-10-04.title: For Irish, E.U. May Stand for Economic Unity
2009-10-04.abstract: Irish voters overwhelmingly approved the European Union’s Lisbon Treaty, but like many other Europeans, they are glumly fixated on domestic issues.

2009-10-05.title: Still Fragile, Haiti Makes Sales Pitch
2009-10-05.abstract: As it tries to lure foreign investors with low costs, Haiti is caught in a paradox: it needs jobs to quell social tensions, but companies require stability to create jobs.

2009-10-05.title: As Authorities Struggle to Help Indonesian Quake Victims, Neighbors Fill the Void
2009-10-05.abstract: In the heart of Padang, with little or sometimes no help coming from the authorities, some affected neighborhoods turned to an informal network of businesses and volunteers.

2009-10-05.title: Slow Recovery After Indonesian Quake
2009-10-05.abstract: Four days after a 7.6-magnitude earthquake hit the western coast of Sumatra, more than 700 people were confirmed dead, and thousands remained missing.

2009-10-05.title: Iran Agrees to Allow Inspectors on Oct. 25
2009-10-05.abstract: The director of the International Atomic Energy Agency said during a visit to Tehran that Iran had given access to a newly disclosed nuclear enrichment facility.

2009-10-05.title: Voice of Bush’s Favored General Is Now Harder to Hear
2009-10-05.abstract: Gen. David H. Petraeus faces a new reality in President Obama’s White House: He is still at the table, but in a very different seat.

2009-10-06.title: Scrambling for Space in Life, and Afterward
2009-10-06.abstract: For the families of the 120,000 people who die annually in Moscow, finding an afterlife dwelling is a challenge.

2009-10-06.title: Afghan President’s Running Mate Poised for a Political Comeback
2009-10-06.abstract: Marshal Muhammad Qasim Fahim has denied charges of drug dealing and corruption that have caused Western officials to have reservations about him.

2009-10-06.title: Contention Over Rankings of African Nations
2009-10-06.abstract: Behind efforts to assess African governments lies a dispute over who should have final editorial say.

2009-10-06.title: France: First Lady’s Web Site Crashes
2009-10-06.abstract: The Web site opened by Carla Sarkozy crashed almost immediately, a problem that was attributed to heavy traffic, but was up again by Monday night.

2009-10-06.title: Israel: Official Cancels Trip to Britain
2009-10-06.abstract: Moshe Yaalon, a government minister and former army chief of staff, feared he could be arrested on war crimes charges.

2009-10-07.title: U.S. Envoy Protests Violence in Guinea
2009-10-07.abstract: Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton said that the military government “cannot remain in power.”

2009-10-07.title: Returned Artifacts Displayed in Kabul
2009-10-07.abstract: Two thousand stolen treasures, some dating from the Bronze Age, were returned to Afghanistan by British authorities in February.

2009-10-07.title: Politicians in India Turn Against One Another
2009-10-07.abstract: The Bharatiya Janata Party, whose mix of Hindu nationalism and reform was once a winning combination, now finds itself divided after election losses.

2009-10-07.title: Giants in Cattle Industry Agree to Help Fight Deforestation
2009-10-07.abstract: Environmental groups hailed a decision by four of the world’s largest meat producers to ban the purchase of cattle from newly deforested areas of Brazil’s Amazon rain forest.

2009-10-07.title: China Aims to Steady North Korea
2009-10-07.abstract: China wants to ward off an implosion by North Korea, which would endanger the stability of its own border area.

2009-10-08.title: Madagascar: Deal on Interim Presidency
2009-10-08.abstract: Andry Rajoelina, who seized power in March with the military’s support, will remain as president of the country’s transitional government, mediators said.

2009-10-08.title: France: Culture Minister Is Attacked for Paying Young Asian Boys for Sex
2009-10-08.abstract: Frédéric Mitterrand, who has defended the filmmaker Roman Polanski against extradition charges for statutory rape, faced calls for his resignation.

2009-10-08.title: Greece: Socialists Are Sworn In
2009-10-08.abstract: Days after crushing conservatives in national elections, Greece’s new Socialist government promised to re-instill faith in government and steer the country out of a recession.

2009-10-08.title: A Palace Overhaul, Treading on French Heritage
2009-10-08.abstract: The planned renovation of Hôtel Lambert, a crumbling 17th-century palace, has French preservationists seeing a cultural threat where some are seeing xenophobia.

2009-10-08.title: Experience Guides Mexico as Swine Flu Cases Surge
2009-10-08.abstract: Health officials have adopted more focused measures than when the strain first appeared nearly six months ago.

2009-10-09.title: Paper Challenges Ideas About ‘Early Bird’ Dinosaur
2009-10-09.abstract: Closer examination of an Archaeopteryx fossil suggests that the species was a feathered dinosaur that did not have the characteristics of a modern bird.

2009-10-09.title: Honduras: Latest Negotiation Efforts Fall Short
2009-10-09.abstract: A delegation of foreign ministers and top diplomats from the Organization of American States left the Honduran capital on Thursday after talks failed to make headway on resolving the country’s political crisis.

2009-10-09.title: Russia: Fight Continues Over Skyscraper Project
2009-10-09.abstract: The prospects for a contested building site, a skyscraper in St. Petersburg for the state energy giant Gazprom, are again unclear after the minister of culture filed papers to block it.

2009-10-09.title: Israel: Foreign Minister Scoffs at Prospects for Peace
2009-10-09.abstract: Foreign Minister Avigdor Lieberman said Thursday that it was unrealistic to expect to reach a final peace agreement with the Palestinians soon.

2009-10-09.title: Man Pleads Guilty in Plot to Bomb Sites in Toronto
2009-10-09.abstract: Zakaria Amara pleaded guilty to leading a plot to blow up prominent sites in a bid to create chaos to force Canada to withdraw its troops from Afghanistan.

2009-10-10.title: For Presidency in Search of Success, Nobel Adds a Twist
2009-10-10.abstract: The Nobel Peace Prize is a reminder of the gap between the ambitious promise of President Obama’s words and his accomplishments.

2009-10-10.title: A Novelist Whose Fiction Comes From Real Lives
2009-10-10.abstract: A. S. Byatt’s latest novel, “The Children’s Book,” is full of characters and takes readers from 1895 until after World War I.

2009-10-10.title: Ecuador Oil Pollution Case Only Grows Murkier
2009-10-10.abstract: A multibillion-dollar legal case between Amazon peasants and Chevron over oil pollution in the rain forest has unfolded like a mystery thriller.

2009-10-10.title: Czech President Objects to Treaty’s Property Rights
2009-10-10.abstract: President Vaclav Klaus said he feared that the provision on property rights could be used as a legal basis for a flood of property claims related to World War II.

2009-10-10.title: World Reacts With Cheer and Caution
2009-10-10.abstract: Following are excerpts from comments by people from around the world on Friday on the awarding of the Nobel Peace Prize to President Obama, gathered by correspondents for The New York Times.

2009-10-11.title: A Schoolgirl’s Odyssey
2009-10-11.abstract: A documentary follows a Pakistani girl, Malala Yousafzai, through a perilous six months as she loses her education, is forced into exile and faces an uncertain return back home in Swat, Pakistan.

2009-10-11.title: Corrections
2009-10-11.abstract: An article last Sunday about extrajudicial killings by the police in India overstated what is known about the cause of a train fire in 2002 that killed Hindu pilgrims in the Indian state of Gujarat, which led to deadly anti-Muslim riots there and may have contributed to what rights activists called an atmosphere in which extrajudicial police killings of Muslim suspects were encouraged. While the Gujarat government concluded the fire was a Muslim attack, that finding has been disputed by a central government investigation, which concluded the fire was an accident.

2009-10-11.title: A Family’s Journey and a Girl’s Dream
2009-10-11.abstract: A six-month journey following a family’s flight from and return to its home in Pakistan’s embattled Swat Valley.

2009-10-11.title: China Sets Sentences in Brawl Tied to Riot
2009-10-11.abstract: One man was sentenced to death and another to life in prison for their roles in a deadly toy factory fight that was blamed for setting off riots in western China’s Xinjiang region.

2009-10-11.title: In Typhoons’ Wake, Filipinos Search for Landslide Victims
2009-10-11.abstract: Successive storms have killed more than 600 people, and the toll is expected to rise as the search continues in mountainous, remote regions.

2009-10-12.title: On Cluttered Ballots of India, Families Proliferate
2009-10-12.abstract: Political dynasties have grown so rapidly that many analysts say nepotism is corroding the democratic system.

2009-10-12.title: Civilian Goals Largely Unmet in Afghanistan
2009-10-12.abstract: Obama administration officials say the U.S. is falling far short of the president’s goals to fight corruption, create a functioning government and train a police force.

2009-10-12.title: Nepotism in Indian Politics
2009-10-12.abstract: Democracy is built on the oft-tarnished ideal that any man or woman can get elected, but in India it helps to be part of a political family.

2009-10-12.title: Car Bomb Kills at Least 41 in Restive Region of Pakistan
2009-10-12.abstract: The blast was the most deadly attack in the Swat Valley since the end of an offensive against militants there over the summer.

2009-10-12.title: China Court Sentences 6 to Death in Rioting
2009-10-12.abstract: All the men, who were convicted of murder, had names that suggested that they were Uighurs, the largest ethnic group in the vast region of Xinjiang.

2009-10-13.title: Pakistan Aid Places U.S. in the Midst of a Divide
2009-10-13.abstract: A new aid bill exposes troubles at the heart of the U.S.-Pakistan alliance and the rocky relationship between Pakistan’s military and its government.

2009-10-13.title: Musician Changes Tone of Impoverished Village
2009-10-13.abstract: One of Brazil’s best-known artists founded a music school and a civic association that transformed his poverty-stricken native village.

2009-10-13.title: Iranian Journalists Flee, Fearing Retribution for Covering Protests
2009-10-13.abstract: Dozens of reporters, photographers and bloggers have either fled Iran or are trying to flee in the aftermath of the disputed June presidential election.

2009-10-13.title: Clinton and Gates Join Forces in Debate on Afghanistan Buildup
2009-10-13.abstract: In a surprising evolution, the secretaries of state and defense have formed an informal partnership in favor of a moderate buildup in American troops.

2009-10-13.title: Afghanistan: Electoral Commission Member Resigns
2009-10-13.abstract: An Afghan member of the agency conducting the audit of the presidential election said he had resigned because the three foreigners on the commission were controlling the decision making.

2009-10-14.title: In Kabul, Little Hope That a Runoff Will Be Fair
2009-10-14.abstract: Many Afghan voters said that a second vote would only lead to another flawed result.

2009-10-14.title: Turkey and Syria Signal Improved Relations
2009-10-14.abstract: The two countries signed agreements to remove visa restrictions on their shared border and pledged to build “a common future.”

2009-10-14.title: Guinea Boasts of Deal With Chinese Company
2009-10-14.abstract: By playing up the agreement, analysts said, the military junta seemed to be trying to counter international condemnation of a mass killing of protesters last month.

2009-10-14.title: Biden No Longer a Lone Voice on Afghanistan
2009-10-14.abstract: Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr.’s pessimism on Afghanistan has caught on with many liberal Democrats.

2009-10-14.title: As Iraq Seeks Oil Investors, They See an Uncertain Bet
2009-10-14.abstract: The Iraqi government is juggling competing demands of attracting foreign investment and guarding its natural resources.

2009-10-15.title: Crossroads of Islam, Past and Present
2009-10-15.abstract: What Western media have portrayed as “the ancestral homeland” of Osama bin Laden is, in reality, a historic center of Sufism, a mystical strand within Islam.

2009-10-15.title: Accounts at Odds on Honduras Deal
2009-10-15.abstract: A negotiator for the ousted leader spoke of progress on a deal that would restore him, but representatives of the leader of the de facto government disputed that report.

2009-10-15.title: British Plan Would Deploy Bigger Afghanistan Force
2009-10-15.abstract: Prime Minister Gordon Brown of Britain hinted strongly that discussions with American leaders had persuaded him that they would approve a modest increase in American troops.

2009-10-15.title: Some See Iran as Ready for Nuclear Deal
2009-10-15.abstract: Analysts say Iran’s leaders may have what they want: the apparent ability to make a weapon, and attention.

2009-10-15.title: Biggest Obstacle to Global Climate Deal May Be How to Pay for It
2009-10-15.abstract: The price of a climate agreement will be $100 billion a year by 2020, many economists estimate; some put the cost at closer to $1 trillion.

2009-10-16.title: Japan Rethinks a Dam, and a Town Protests
2009-10-16.abstract: Bridge supports cross a reservoir behind the Yamba Dam, a project the new Japanese government has suspended.

2009-10-16.title: An Isolated Village Finds the Energy to Keep Going
2009-10-16.abstract: Visitors to a remote village in Colombia can get a glimpse into a four-decade experiment to alter civilization’s dependence on finite fossil fuels and industrial agriculture.

2009-10-16.title: A Moderate Path in a bin Laden Birthplace
2009-10-16.abstract: What Western media have portrayed as Osama bin Laden's ancestral homeland is a historic center of Sufism, a mystical strand within Islam.

2009-10-16.title: Madagascar: Factions Will Negotiate
2009-10-16.abstract: Leaders of the island’s four main political groups are scheduled to meet in Geneva this weekend to try again to agree on key transitional government posts.

2009-10-16.title: Five Convicted in Australia Terror Plot
2009-10-16.abstract: Prosecutors said the defendants stockpiled dangerous weapons to wage Islamist jihad against the Australian government.

2009-10-17.title: Madagascar: Power-Sharing Talks Off
2009-10-17.abstract: This weekend’s meeting between the island’s four main political groups has been canceled, United Nations mediators announced.

2009-10-17.title: Britain: High Court Approves Releasing U.S. Intelligence Documents on Torture
2009-10-17.abstract: A panel of the High Court ruled that American intelligence documents containing details pertinent to torture allegations by a former detainee at Guantánamo Bay should be made public.

2009-10-17.title: Botswana: Leader Expected to Win Vote
2009-10-17.abstract: Seretse Khama Ian Khama is expected to win a general election held Friday, though Botswana has been hit hard by the global economic crisis.

2009-10-17.title: Russia: 2 Opposition Parties Return
2009-10-17.abstract: Two days after they walked out of Russia’s Parliament to protest what they said was widespread election fraud, two opposition parties returned, but a third did not.

2009-10-17.title: Turkey: Show Stays on TV After Protest
2009-10-17.abstract: A television show on a state-run channel in Turkey that appears to portray Israeli Army hostility against Palestinians will continue with new episodes.

2009-10-18.title: Iran Frees Reporter Held Since June
2009-10-18.abstract: Maziar Bahari, an Iranian-Canadian Newsweek journalist, had been arrested in postelection unrest and put on trial in August with about 100 other detainees.

2009-10-18.title: Sudan’s Critics Relieved That Obama Chose a Middle Course
2009-10-18.abstract: Advocates of a tougher approach toward Khartoum said they had feared the White House would take an even more conciliatory line toward the government.

2009-10-18.title: Iran Guard Commanders Are Killed in Bombings
2009-10-18.abstract: At least five commanders of Iran’s elite Revolutionary Guards were killed and dozens of others left dead and injured as two bombs hit near Iran’s frontier with Pakistan, according to state news agencies.

2009-10-18.title: By Air and Ground, Pakistani Soldiers Penetrate Militant Heartland
2009-10-18.abstract: The Pakistani military moved deeper into South Waziristan, hitting Taliban targets with F-16 fighter jets as troops supported by helicopter gunships climbed higher into the terrain.

2009-10-18.title: Button Wins Title, Webber Wins Race
2009-10-18.abstract: Button wins title, Webber wins race.

2009-10-19.title: Amid a Crisis, Hondurans Heap Large Hopes on a Tiny Religious Icon
2009-10-19.abstract: Many Hondurans are looking to Our Lady of Suyapa, their country’s patron saint who is revered for her power to work miracles, to help put the country back on track.

2009-10-19.title: Decision on Afghan Troops May Wait
2009-10-19.abstract: Officials said President Obama could postpone any decision on sending more troops until the disputed election has been settled.

2009-10-19.title: Many Sources Feed Taliban’s War Chest
2009-10-19.abstract: Despite efforts by the U.S. and its allies to cripple the Taliban’s sophisticated financial network, American officials say they’ve barely made a dent.

2009-10-19.title: Corrections
2009-10-19.abstract: An article last Monday about the emigration or attempted emigration of dozens of Iranian reporters, photographers and bloggers in the aftermath of the disputed June presidential election in Iran misstated the given name of a photographer who applied for asylum in Turkey. He is Javad Moghimi, not Reza.

2009-10-19.title: Q. and A.: Held by the Taliban
2009-10-19.abstract: This week, David Rohde, a New York Times reporter, and Bill Keller, the executive editor, are answering readers’ questions on Mr. Rohde’s seven months as a captive of the Taliban in Pakistan.

2009-10-20.title: China: Ex-Professor Given 10-Year Term
2009-10-20.abstract: Guo Quan, who established an independent political party, was sentenced for “subversion of state power,” according to Human Rights in China, an advocacy group.

2009-10-20.title: South Africa: 4 Students Expelled Over Video Are Allowed Back in School
2009-10-20.abstract: The four students had made a video that humiliated black cleaning women through a mock initiation ritual.

2009-10-20.title: No Prize to Ex-Head of State This Year
2009-10-20.abstract: A committee that awards an annual $5 million prize for good governance to former African heads of state decided not to do so this year because it could not select a winner.

2009-10-20.title: Iraqi Campus Is Under Gang’s Sway
2009-10-20.abstract: One of Iraq’s most prestigious universities was closed temporarily because of a student gang accused of murdering and raping students and professors.

2009-10-20.title: White House’s New Sudan Strategy Fits Envoy’s Pragmatic Style
2009-10-20.abstract: Critics rebuked the new policy as naïve while supporters said it reflected common sense. But both camps agree it has Maj. Gen. J. Scott Gration’s fingerprints all over it.

2009-10-21.title: Lifting the Lid on Russia’s Art of Lavish Gift Giving
2009-10-21.abstract: Until recently, Aleksandr Y. Khochinsky specialized in providing high-class grease for the best-connected palms in Russia.

2009-10-21.title: AIDS Vaccine Trial Shows Only Slight Protection
2009-10-21.abstract: The full results from a trial in Thailand showed that the vaccine’s protective effect might be even weaker than researchers first admitted.

2009-10-21.title: Held by the Taliban
2009-10-21.abstract: For seven months, The Times's David Rohde was held captive by the Taliban. Much of what he learned during that time came from watching videos- day after day- with his captors.

2009-10-21.title: Botswana: Leader Starts 2nd Term
2009-10-21.abstract: President Seretse Khama Ian Khama was sworn in Tuesday after his party, the Botswana Democratic Party, won a landslide electoral victory.

2009-10-21.title: South Africa: Warning on Violence
2009-10-21.abstract: President Jacob Zuma condemned violent demonstrations against poor public services on Tuesday as a violation of the country’s history of protest.

2009-10-22.title: Iran: 5 Executions Include Woman
2009-10-22.abstract: Human rights groups have documented a sharp increase in the number of executions in Iran since the start of the year.

2009-10-22.title: Iranian-American Stunned by Sentence
2009-10-22.abstract: Kian Tajbakhsh, who was arrested in postelection protests in Iran and sentenced to 15 years in prison on Sunday, had been hopeful that he would be released, a family member says.

2009-10-22.title: Offer Raises Idea of Marriage for Catholic Priests
2009-10-22.abstract: The invitation to join the Catholic Church extends to married Anglican clergy, leading some to wonder whether the move could liberalize the church on a crucial issue: celibacy.

2009-10-22.title: Iran’s Politics Open a Generational Chasm
2009-10-22.abstract: Since Iran’s disputed presidential election in June and the protests that followed, many children of prominent Iranians have become estranged from their parents.

2009-10-22.title: Canada: Standoff Ends Peacefully
2009-10-22.abstract: A man armed with a rifle surrendered to the police in Edmonton, Alberta, after holding several people hostage at a government office for about 10 hours.

2009-10-23.title: A New Vote Poses Similar Troubles for Afghans
2009-10-23.abstract: The serious fraud in Afghanistan’s presidential election last summer is unlikely to be repeated on the same scale in the runoff set for Nov. 7.

2009-10-23.title: Fear Over Swine Flu Closes Schools in Iraq
2009-10-23.abstract: Fear of the H1N1 virus has prompted nearly 2,500 school closings throughout Iraq, in what some health officials have called an overreaction.

2009-10-23.title: Iranian Lawmaker Dismisses Uranium Plan
2009-10-23.abstract: The deputy speaker of Iran’s Parliament appeared to reject a draft plan to have Iran ship its uranium abroad for processing.

2009-10-23.title: Mideast Gain Is Modest, Clinton Tells President
2009-10-23.abstract: Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton reported to the president that modest progress had been made in the month since he met with leaders of Israel and the Palestinian Authority.

2009-10-23.title: Sarkozy’s Son Ends Effort to Oversee Business District
2009-10-23.abstract: Jean Sarkozy, 23, had hoped to run the organization that oversees La Défense, a business district on the western edge of Paris. But cries of nepotism thwarted his bid.

2009-10-24.title: Iran Is Said to Arrest Wives of Many Prominent Detainees
2009-10-24.abstract: The authorities raided a prayer ceremony in Tehran, reformist Web sites reported, and a witness said 60 people were taken into custody.

2009-10-24.title: Czech Republic: Deal on Charter
2009-10-24.abstract: President Vaclav Klaus on Friday welcomed a compromise aimed at easing his concerns about the European Union’s Lisbon Treaty.

2009-10-24.title: Russia: Assassination Attempt Fails
2009-10-24.abstract: Chechen forces foiled an attempt on the life of President Ramzan A. Kadyrov and his closest ally, Adam Delimkhanov, on Friday.

2009-10-24.title: Thailand: Rights Panel Formed
2009-10-24.abstract: Southeast Asian governments meeting in Thailand inaugurated their first human rights commission on Friday in what they hailed as a milestone for a region.

2009-10-24.title: Nigeria: Help for Fighting Malaria
2009-10-24.abstract: The Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria announced Friday that Nigeria would receive grants to distribute 30 million antimalarial bed nets.

2009-10-25.title: Bombings in Iraq, Deadliest Since 2007, Raise Security Issue
2009-10-25.abstract: Sunday’s bombings, which killed over 155 people, poses a challenge to Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki, whose popularity has rested on the belief that he has kept Iraq safe.

2009-10-25.title: Bill to Increase Access to Contraception Is Dividing Filipinos
2009-10-25.abstract: Family planning advocates trying to make birth control more readily available are meeting resistance in the overwhelmingly Roman Catholic country.

2009-10-25.title: Over 130 Dead in Baghdad Suicide Attacks
2009-10-25.abstract: The bloodiest attack in Baghdad in two months damaged two government buildings and resulted in the deaths of over 130.

2009-10-25.title: Ingushetia Opposition Figure Is Shot Dead in Car
2009-10-25.abstract: The death of Maksharip Aushev, a businessman from a prominent family, added another name to the list of activists who have been killed recently in the Northern Caucasus.

2009-10-25.title: Israeli Police Clash With Palestinians at Sacred Compound in Jerusalem
2009-10-25.abstract: Twenty-six people were hurt at the Temple Mount compound in the latest sign of tension in Jerusalem.

2009-10-26.title: Michael Moore Irks Supporters of Chávez
2009-10-26.abstract: Apparently tongue-in-cheeck comments by the documentary filmmaker about President Hugo Chávez have created an uproar among some Chavistas.

2009-10-26.title: Despite Switch, Mexico City’s Lights Flicker
2009-10-26.abstract: President Felipe Calderón had Mexico City’s money-losing electric company dissolved and promised good service. But service has been out at times for millions.

2009-10-26.title: Afghan Rivals Rule Out Power-Sharing Deal
2009-10-26.abstract: President Hamid Karzai ’s announcement appeared to reflect his confidence that he would win the runoff handily and that there was no need to form a coalition with Abdullah Abdullah.

2009-10-26.title: Asian Leaders Focus on Freer Trade
2009-10-26.abstract: Leaders expressed cautious optimism about increasing trade within the region, lowering tariffs and plans for a wider Asian free-trade zone.

2009-10-26.title: Both Iran and West Fear a Trap on Deal
2009-10-26.abstract: Iran is afraid of falling for a Western ruse to neutralize its “strategic reserve” while the West fears being lured into a plot to buy time for a nuclear bomb program.

2009-10-27.title: U.S. Sending Envoys to Try to End Crisis in Honduras
2009-10-27.abstract: The stepped-up pressure comes after months of apparently fruitless talks about whether Manuel Zelaya, the ousted president, will be returned to power.

2009-10-27.title: No Rushing on Decisions, Obama Says
2009-10-27.abstract: The president’s speech to a military audience offered few clues about the review of his policy toward Afghanistan and Pakistan.

2009-10-27.title: Opportunities Fade Amid Sense of Isolation in Gaza
2009-10-27.abstract: The ideological differences between Fatah and Hamas have frustrated Palestinian professionals, who are seeing fewer prospects for education and employment.

2009-10-27.title: German Limits on War Face Afghan Reality
2009-10-27.abstract: The German Army has been engaged in fighting in Afghanistan on a scale not seen since World War II, just as the war effort grows unpopular at home.

2009-10-27.title: For Germany, a Grim Reality
2009-10-27.abstract: Forced to confront the rising insurgency in once peaceful northern Afghanistan, the German Army is now fighting and killing on a scale it has not seen since World War II.

2009-10-28.title: Brother of Afghan Leader Said to Be Paid by C.I.A.
2009-10-28.abstract: Ahmed Wali Karzai, the brother of President Hamid Karzai and a suspected player in the opium trade, has gotten payments from the C.I.A. for eight years, American officials said.

2009-10-28.title: Celebrating María Lionza
2009-10-28.abstract: Each October, thousands of followers of the María Lionza religion make a pilgrimage to Sorte Mountain in Venezuela for a weekend of fire dances, cleansing ceremonies and healing rituals.

2009-10-28.title: In Venezuela, Adoration Meets Blend of Traditions
2009-10-28.abstract: Each October, devotees of the María Lionza religion perform rituals to honor the spirits or ask for favors.

2009-10-28.title: State Department Official Turned Lobbyist Is Accused of Illegally Working for Sudan
2009-10-28.abstract: The former official, Robert J. Cabelly, was charged with acting as an unregistered agent of a foreign country, money laundering, passport fraud and making false statements.

2009-10-28.title: Greece: Afghan Refugees Drown
2009-10-28.abstract: Eight Afghan refugees, including five minors and three women, drowned in choppy waters in the northeast Aegean Sea on Tuesday when the rickety craft that was ferrying them to Greece sank.

2009-10-29.title: In Cancún, Police Pick Wrong People for ‘Bite’
2009-10-29.abstract: When police in Cancún, Mexico, extorted $300 from an American couple, they didn’t know that the driver’s wife was a Minnesota state senator who would fight back.

2009-10-29.title: Pervasive Corruption Rattles Iraq’s Fragile State
2009-10-29.abstract: As Americans play less of a direct role in security operations, corruption is more of a threat.

2009-10-29.title: Chilean President Rides High as Term Ends
2009-10-29.abstract: After a rough start, Michelle Bachelet, Chile’s first woman in the presidency, is winding up her term as one of the country’s most popular leaders.

2009-10-29.title: U.S. Embargo on Cuba Again Finds Scant Support at U.N.
2009-10-29.abstract: The General Assembly voted overwhelmingly to condemn the American trade embargo against Cuba, reflecting that little has changed despite an expected shift under the Obama administration.

2009-10-29.title: A Pilgrimage to Sorte Mountain
2009-10-29.abstract: Devotees of the María Lionza religion journey each October to a mountain in Venezuela, home of the high altar to the religion’s central figure.

2009-10-30.title: Crimean Mosque Project Stirs Debate and Trauma
2009-10-30.abstract: A plan to build a mosque for Crimean Tatars has stirred up a dispute involving politics, communal grievances and international tensions.

2009-10-30.title: Mozambique: President’s Re-election Seems Certain
2009-10-30.abstract: President Armando E. Guebueza of the governing party, Frelimo, appeared to be on his way to re-election in a landslide, according to early and unofficial results announced Thursday on Radio Mozambique.

2009-10-30.title: As Donors Focus on AIDS, Child Illnesses Languish
2009-10-30.abstract: Diarrhea and pneumonia kill millions of children and are easily treatable, but wealthy nations’ focus remains on the costly fight against AIDS.

2009-10-30.title: Canada: 6 Bikers Convicted in Murders of 8 Others
2009-10-30.abstract: A jury in London, Ontario, convicted six men on Thursday of murdering eight fellow members of the Bandidos motorcycle gang.

2009-10-30.title: China: Repairs on TV Tower to Begin Soon
2009-10-30.abstract: Nearly nine months ago, a stray fireworks rocket set alight a 520-foot tower during Lunar New Year celebrations, destroying part of downtown Beijing’s massive new state television headquarters.

2009-10-31.title: Mozambique Is Reporting Big Victory for President
2009-10-31.abstract: A partial count in Mozambique’s elections showed President Armando E. Guebuza of the Frelimo party likely to finish with around 75 percent of the votes.

2009-10-31.title: Insurgent Attacks Kill 12 in Afghanistan
2009-10-31.abstract: Nine travelers in a station wagon, including a woman and a child, were killed in Nangahar Province, according to the spokesman for the provincial governor.

2009-10-31.title: John Burns on Ahmed Wali Karzai and the C.I.A.
2009-10-31.abstract: John Burns responds to comments on The New York Times report on Ahmed Wali Karzai’s alleged drug ties and his relationship with the C.I.A.

2009-10-31.title: Obama Meets Joint Chiefs to Review Afghanistan Strategy
2009-10-31.abstract: The top officers from the Army, Marine Corps, Navy and Air Force briefed the president on the long-term consequences for personnel and equipment under various options being considered.

2009-10-31.title: Somalia: Pirates Seek $7 Million for British Couple
2009-10-31.abstract: The BBC reported that a man claiming to be a spokesman for Somali pirates had demanded a $7 million ransom for a British couple seized from their 38-foot sailboat off the Seychelles a week ago.
